internet.q: Which part of a URL tells the browser which protocol to use?
internet.explain: The scheme (https://) tells the browser which protocol to use to talk to the server.

internet.q: A page generated fresh from a database on every request is called…
internet.explain: Dynamic sites build content at request time, often from a database.

opts: Maps domain names to IP addresses
internet.explain: DNS translates human-readable domain names into numeric IP addresses.

clientserver.q: Which HTTP status code means the resource wasn't found?
clientserver.explain: 404 Not Found means the server has no resource at the requested URL.
The client (browser) always sends the first request.

clientserver.q: Which HTTP method is typically used to submit form data that changes server state?
clientserver.explain: POST is conventionally used for submitting data that creates or changes something server-side.

html5.q: Which of these is an HTML5 semantic element?
html5.explain: <article> describes self-contained content, a semantic addition in HTML5.

html5.q: What does the HTML5 DOCTYPE declaration do?
opts: Puts the browser in standards mode for HTML5
html5.explain: <!DOCTYPE html> tells the browser to render using modern HTML5 rules.

html5.q: Which HTML5 feature removed the need for Flash in most video embeds?
html5.explain: The native <video> element plays media without third-party plugins.

structure.q: Which element holds content the visitor actually sees?

opts: In the browser tab and bookmarks
structure.explain: <title> sets the text shown in the browser tab and used for bookmarks/search results.

structure.q: Which attribute on <html> declares the page's language?
structure.explain: lang='en' (etc.) declares the document language for accessibility and SEO.

elements.q: Which attribute must be unique on the page?
elements.explain: id identifies exactly one element; class can repeat across many elements.

elements.q: Which of these is an empty (void) tag?
elements.explain: has no separate closing tag — it's a void element.

elements.q: Which of these is a block-level element by default?
elements.explain: <div> starts on a new line by default, unlike inline elements like <span> or <a>.

attributes.q: Which attribute adds a tooltip shown on hover?
attributes.explain: title text is displayed as a native tooltip on hover.

attributes.q: What prefix identifies a custom attribute intended for scripting?
attributes.explain: data-* attributes are the standard way to attach custom data to an element.

attributes.q: Which attribute disables a form control?
attributes.explain: disabled prevents interaction and excludes the field from form submission.

text.q: Which tag is used for the single most important heading on a page?
text.explain: <h1> is the top-level heading; there should usually be exactly one per page.

text.q: Which tag preserves whitespace and line breaks exactly as written?
text.explain: <pre> renders text in a fixed-width font and keeps spacing/line breaks intact.

text.q: Which tag semantically highlights text, like a highlighter pen?
text.explain: <mark> denotes text highlighted for reference or relevance.

lists.q: Which element pairs a term with its definition?
lists.explain: <dl> uses <dt> for the term and <dd> for its definition.

lists.q: How do you create a sub-list inside a list item?
lists.explain: Nesting a list element directly inside an <li> creates a sub-list.

lists.q: Which attribute changes what number an ordered list starts at?
lists.explain: start='5' makes the list begin counting at 5.

links.q: Which href value creates an email link?
links.explain: The mailto: scheme opens the visitor's default email client.

links.q: Which attribute combination safely opens an external link in a new tab?
links.explain: rel='noopener' prevents the new tab from accessing the opening page via window.opener.

links.q: What does an href value starting with # link to?
opts: A section on the same page
links.explain: A #fragment links to an element with a matching id on the current page.

images.q: What does the alt attribute do?
images.explain: alt text is shown if the image fails to load and read aloud by screen readers.

images.q: Which attribute lets the browser pick the best image for the screen size?
images.explain: srcset lists multiple image candidates so the browser can choose the best fit.

images.q: What is the purpose of loading='lazy' on an ?
opts: Defer loading offscreen images until needed
images.explain: Lazy loading defers fetching images until they're near the viewport, improving performance.

svg.q: Why use SVG instead of a JPEG/PNG for simple shapes?
opts: SVG stays crisp at any size
svg.explain: SVG describes shapes mathematically, so it scales without pixelating.

svg.q: Which element draws a straight line in SVG?
svg.explain: <line x1 y1 x2 y2> draws a straight line between two points.

svg.q: What does the viewBox attribute control?
opts: The coordinate system shapes are drawn in
svg.explain: viewBox defines the internal coordinate space, independent of the element's display size.

audio.q: Which attribute shows native play/pause controls?
audio.explain: controls displays the browser's built-in audio UI.

audio.q: Why is muted often required alongside autoplay?
opts: Browsers block autoplaying sound without it
audio.explain: Most browsers block autoplay with sound unless the media is muted.

audio.q: What does the loop attribute do on <audio>?
audio.explain: loop restarts playback automatically when the track ends.

video.q: Which attribute shows a preview image before a video plays?
video.explain: poster sets the image shown before playback starts.

video.q: Which element adds captions to a video?
video.explain: <track kind='captions'> links a caption file (e.g. WebVTT) to the video.

video.q: Why include width and height on a <video>?
opts: To reserve layout space and prevent content shifting
video.explain: Explicit dimensions reserve space before the video loads, avoiding layout shift.

opts: Embeds another page/document inside the current one
iframes.explain: <iframe> nests another HTML document inside the current page.

iframes.q: Why does an iframe need a title attribute?
iframes.explain: title gives assistive technology a readable label for the embedded content.

iframes.q: What does the sandbox attribute do on an iframe?
opts: Restricts what the embedded page is allowed to do
iframes.explain: sandbox applies a set of restrictions (like disabling scripts) to embedded, less-trusted content.

seo.q: What does the meta description tag control?
opts: The snippet shown under the title in search results
seo.explain: meta description supplies the summary text search engines often display.

seo.q: Why does alt text help SEO?
opts: It gives search engines readable text describing the image
seo.explain: Search engines can't 'see' images — alt text gives them (and screen readers) something to read.

seo.q: What does a canonical link tag prevent?
seo.explain: rel='canonical' tells search engines which URL is the authoritative version.

bestpractices.q: How many <h1> elements should a typical page have?
bestpractices.explain: A single <h1> gives the page one clear main heading for users and assistive tech.

bestpractices.q: Which is a best practice for long-term maintainability?
opts: Skipping alt text to save space
bestpractices.explain: Semantic structure and readable indentation make markup easier to debug and extend.

bestpractices.q: What does running markup through a validator help catch?
bestpractices.explain: Validators check markup against the HTML spec, flagging structural errors.
